summary: Backend-only verification of the bug-fix bundle (compliance_enabled on Firm Master, /api/salary/monthly auto-pending payslips, /api/payslips/{id}/mark-paid, /api/admin/payroll) + static inspection of frontend claims. 15/15 pytest assertions passing (6 live auth-gating curls against the public URL + 9 static server.py assertions). One real bug found and fixed inline: POST /api/companies constructed Company(...) without forwarding payload.compliance_enabled, so any 'Show compliance documents = false' from the UI was silently reverted to True on create. Added compliance_enabled=payload.compliance_enabled to the Company(...) constructor and restarted backend; retest passes. Frontend claims verified via grep/inspection: testIDs company-address-input, search-address-btn, search-results, search-result-{i}, company-lat-input, company-lng-input, toggle-compliance, seg-compliance (conditionally rendered when compliance_enabled !== false with auto-fallback to payslips tab), salary-card (rendered on payslips tab when salaryMonthly is truthy), styles.salaryLine in admin.tsx under e.salary_monthly, payroll.tsx with seg-pending/seg-paid + mark-paid-{slip_id} + isAdmin guard, and row-payroll gated behind isAdmin in profile.tsx. Regression: landing-screen + google-login-button testIDs present, auth-error-banner only rendered when authError is truthy, onboarding name is useState('') with NO useEffect syncing from user.name.

minor.issue: Fixed inline: was dropping payload.compliance_enabled when constructing Company(...), so UI toggle 'Show compliance documents = off' was silently overridden to true on create. Added compliance_enabled=payload.compliance_enabled to the Company(...) call in create_company. PATCH /api/companies/{id} was already forwarding it correctly via CompanyUpdate.

action_items: None blocking. Optional: also allow PATCH /api/company (self-serve) to update compliance_enabled — currently only super-admin PATCH /api/companies/{id} accepts it (see server.py update_company allowed set).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py create_company was dropping compliance_enabled from the request (Company defaults it to True). Fixed by forwarding payload.compliance_enabled — verified via pytest.

server.py update_company (PATCH /api/company for company_admin self-serve) has an allowed set of {name,address,office_lat,office_lng,geofence_radius_m} and does NOT include compliance_enabled. Not required by the review request (only super-admin edits it), but flagging for future consistency.
salary/monthly is idempotent per (employee_user_id, month) via find_one before insert — correct.
admin/payroll correctly scopes to company_admin's own company_id and lets super_admin pass ?company_id=; attaches employee_name/employee_email via a batched users.find({user_id:{$in:...}}).
documents.tsx correctly forces tab back to 'payslips' when compliance_enabled === false (line 47) and gates the seg-compliance button (line 80).
frontend uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (correct Expo public-env convention) — no change needed.
seed_data_creation: None. Backend endpoints are gated behind Emergent Google OAuth; live E2E would need a real Google session which is out of scope per review note.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug-fix bundle verified. Live functional testing of salary/monthly auto-payslip generation, payroll list attachment of employee_name/email, and mark-paid role gating still requires an authenticated Emergent OAuth session — reuse a super_admin/company_admin bearer if one becomes available and hit: GET /api/salary/monthly (expect history[] with pending slips for last 6 completed months if user.salary_monthly>0), GET /api/admin/payroll?status=pending, PATCH /api/payslips/{id}/mark-paid.
rca_of_issue: BUG 1 root cause: create_company only wired name/address/office_lat/office_lng/geofence_radius_m into Company(...). Because Company.compliance_enabled defaults to True, any payload with compliance_enabled=false was silently discarded on insert. Reproduction: POST /api/companies with body {name, office_lat, office_lng, compliance_enabled:false} as super_admin would result in a stored doc with compliance_enabled=true. Mitigation applied: pass compliance_enabled=payload.compliance_enabled into the Company constructor. PATCH path (edit_company) was already fine — it flattens CompanyUpdate.model_dump() and $sets everything non-None, so toggling from the edit modal already worked.
